role_title: Staff Technical Program Manager, Guest & Host Tech

location.office_time_required: occasional work at an Airbnb office or attendance at offsites, as agreed to with your manager

level_signals: 10+ years of experience in the software development industry
at least 7 years as a TPM or relevant experience
Comp range $200K-$248K base, consistent with Staff-level IC at Airbnb
Expected to work independently with stakeholders and cross-functional leadership with little supervision

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of experience in the software development industry, with at least 7 years as a TPM or relevant experience .
with at least 7 years as a TPM or relevant experience .

Experience scaling yourself to effectively run complex, highly cross-functional projects where communication and influence with multiple teams and stakeholders inside and outside of engineering is imperative.
Experience managing software projects with a high degree of scrutiny from C-suite execs.
Proven technical domain expert for your projects.  Able to effectively represent engineering decisions/solutions to other teams.
Proven thought partner to Engineering Management or Eng Leads for your domain.
Machine Learning, Data Science experience including creating unique data sets, managing evolving, real-time pipeline architecture.
Demonstrated ability to work through ambiguity to detailed solutions. You can take a vague problem, crisply frame the challenge, identify a path forward, and drive an initiative over the finish-line.
Self-motivated, proactive and ability to work independently with minimal oversight.
Detail-oriented and able to understand the bigger picture by using your technical expertise and problem solving abilities to prioritize and manage blocking issues.
Ability to ramp up quickly and learn new technologies with minimal lag time.
Exhibit knowledge of Asana, Jira, Airtable or other equivalent task management tooling.
Experience leveraging AI to automate processes and scale yourself to cover a larger project domain

scope.surface: Guest & Host Tech — homepage, search, booking, planning, pre-trip/on-trip/post-trip guest experience, host tools; Homes, Experiences, multiple Homes, Hotels verticals
scope.business_model: two-sided marketplace (guests and hosts), consumer-facing and host-facing product surfaces
primary_metrics_inferred: Key performance indicators for program health
core_responsibilities: Develop program scope and objectives, collaborate with all relevant stakeholders, ensure technical feasibility and drive projects end-to-end
Aligning on staffing, priorities and technical dependencies within Guest & Host Tech and cross-functional teams like Payments, Trust Eng, Design and Product

Manage Technical Design diligence process for large programs aligning design backlog w/ project plan, scheduling reviews and pre-reviews, engaging the right stakeholders across teams, and managing meeting quality and efficiency
Manage effective communications to leadership and across teams on key decisions, status, and risks to ensure accountability and timeline management
Understand best practices, drive for process improvements, and find new ways of operating, with a focus on simplification and productivity
Provide visibility into key performance indicators so leaders are aware of risks and opportunities
Work independently with stakeholders and cross-functional leadership with little supervision from management

compensation.equity_form: bonus, equity, benefits, and Employee Travel Credits mentioned

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly stated and competitive ($200K-$248K base)
Clear product surface area described (Guest & Host Tech with named verticals)
Bonus, equity, and Employee Travel Credits mentioned as additional compensation
Inclusive hiring language and disability accommodation process explicitly stated
red_flags: ML/Data Science is listed as a hard requirement ('Machine Learning, Data Science experience including creating unique data sets, managing evolving, real-time pipeline architecture'), which is atypical for TPM roles and may exclude strong PM/TPM generalists
Must reside in a US state where Airbnb has a registered entity; excluded states list is evolving — candidates must verify eligibility
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned; work auth left unclear
10+ years total + 7+ years as TPM is a narrow bar that limits candidate pool significantly

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 'Staff' title, years requirements, and comp range)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description as two-sided marketplace)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly named in JD)
location.work_auth (inferred as no-sponsorship; not explicitly stated but no sponsorship mentioned)
ambiguities: ML/Data Science experience is listed in the 'Your Expertise' required section without 'preferred' or 'nice to have' qualifier — treated as hard requirement, but it is unusually technical for a TPM role and may be aspirational
Visa sponsorship is never mentioned; work_auth set to 'no-sponsorship' is inferred from absence of any sponsorship language, which may not be accurate
The JD says 'must live in a state where Airbnb, Inc. has a registered entity' but does not list excluded states inline — eligibility is contingent on an external link
